reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures the informal claim. "Transitive permutation group on the finite set A" is encoded by `[Fintype α]`, `G : Subgroup (Equiv.Perm α)` (permutation group), and `h_tran : ∀ a b, ∃ σ : G, σ a = b` (standard transitivity). The cardinality hypothesis `Fintype.card α > 1` exactly matches $|A| > 1$. The conclusion `∃ σ : G, ∀ a, σ a ≠ a` is a verbatim translation of "there is some σ ∈ G such that σ(a) ≠ a for all a ∈ A" — i.e., the existence of a fixed-point-free (derangement) element. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and quantifier structure matches exactly.
